2604.15151.title: QuantCode-Bench: A Benchmark for Evaluating the Ability of Large Language Models to Generate Executable Algorithmic Trading Strategies
2604.15151.paper_summary: Large language models have demonstrated strong performance on general-purpose programming tasks, yet their ability to generate executable algorithmic trading strategies remains underexplored. Unlike standard code benchmarks, trading-strategy generation requires simultaneous mastery of domain-specific financial logic, knowledge of a specialized API, and the ability to produce code that is not only syntactically correct but also leads to actual trades on historical data. In this work, we present QuantCode-Bench, a benchmark for the systematic evaluation of modern LLMs in generating strategies for the Backtrader framework from textual descriptions in English. The benchmark contains 400 tasks of varying difficulty collected from Reddit, TradingView, StackExchange, GitHub, and synthetic sources. Evaluation is conducted through a multi-stage pipeline that checks syntactic correctness, successful backtest execution, the presence of trades, and semantic alignment with the task description using an LLM judge. We compare state-of-the-art models in two settings: single-turn, where the strategy must be generated correctly on the first attempt, and agentic multi-turn, where the model receives iterative feedback and may repair its errors. We analyze the failure modes across different stages of the pipeline and show that the main limitations of current models are not related to syntax, but rather to the correct operationalization of trading logic, proper API usage, and adherence to task semantics. These findings suggest that trading strategy generation constitutes a distinct class of domain-specific code generation tasks in which success requires not only technical correctness, but also alignment between natural-language descriptions, financial logic, and the observable behavior of the strategy on data.
2604.15151.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型在通用編程任務上展現了強大的表現，但它們生成可執行的算法交易策略的能力仍然未被充分探索。與標準代碼基準不同，交易策略生成需要同時掌握特定領域的金融邏輯、專業API的知識，以及生成不僅語法正確且能在歷史數據上實際進行交易的代碼的能力。在本研究中，我們提出了QuantCode-Bench，這是一個系統評估現代LLM在從英語文本描述生成Backtrader框架策略方面的基準。該基準包含從Reddit、TradingView、StackExchange、GitHub和合成來源收集的400個不同難度的任務。評估通過多階段流程進行，該流程檢查語法正確性、成功的回測執行、交易的存在以及與任務描述的語義一致性，並使用LLM評判。我们在两种设置中比较最先进的模型：单轮，其中策略必须在第一次尝试时正确生成，以及代理多轮，其中模型接收迭代反馈并可以修复其错误。我们分析了流程不同阶段的失败模式，并显示当前模型的主要限制与语法无关，而是与交易逻辑的正确操作、适当的API使用和对任务语义的遵循有关。这些发现表明，交易策略生成构成了一类独特的特定领域代码生成任务，其中成功不仅需要技术上的正确性，还需要自然语言描述、金融逻辑和策略在数据上的可观察行为之间的一致性。
2604.15151.author: Alexey Khoroshilov et.al.
2604.15151.authors: Alexey Khoroshilov, Alexey Chernysh, Orkhan Ekhtibarov, Nini Kamkia, Dmitry Zmitrovich
